task_completion.description: The agent opened and read the preliminary construction schedule brief.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or document viewer is open showing the contents of 'contractor_schedule_brief.txt' from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The agent created a work schedule in Bonsai/BlenderBIM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai/BlenderBIM interface shows the Sequence/Scheduling panel with a new schedule created (expected name: 'FZK-Haus Construction Programme').

task_completion.description: The agent added at least 5 construction tasks with assigned durations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The task list in the scheduling panel displays 5 or more distinct tasks (e.g., representing major build phases) with visible durations, start dates, or finish dates.

task_completion.description: The agent linked physical building elements to the construction tasks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The interface shows 3D elements (like walls, slabs, windows) assigned to tasks. This can be seen in the 'Assigned Objects', 'Products', or 'Elements' sub-panel of a selected task, or elements highlighting in the 3D viewport when a task is selected.

task_completion.description: The agent saved the completed IFC project to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' or export dialog in Blender showing the file being saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_construction_schedule.ifc', or a file manager window showing the saved file in that location.

integrity.description: The agent used the Bonsai/BlenderBIM graphical user interface to create the schedule and assign elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with Blender's panels, buttons, and 3D viewport, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts modifying the IFC file directly.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely selected 3D model elements to link to tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewport shows building elements being actively selected (highlighted in orange/yellow) before being assigned to the schedule tasks.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using the application's native save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender/Bonsai save project button or file menu is used, rather than terminal commands like 'cp' or 'touch' to fake the output file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected schedule name to be created in Bonsai/BlenderBIM is 'FZK-Haus Construction Programme'. The schedule must contain a minimum of 5 tasks. The FZK-Haus model contains walls, doors, windows, and slabs; look for these specific elements being linked to the tasks. The final output must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_construction_schedule.ifc'. The brief is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/contractor_schedule_brief.txt'.
